title: Hutongs And Lakes
subtitle: Shichahai / towers / Wudaoying
blurb: Walk from Prince Kung Mansion to the lakes, towers, and Wudaoying, cutting noisy stretches through side lanes.
mood: Alleys, water, and frequent choices on the ground

microcopy.line: Slow down at doors and corners, and cut ordinary long stretches.

content.intro:
Start at Prince Kung Mansion, then continue to Shichahai, Yandai Xiejie, the Drum and Bell Towers, and Wudaoying. Do not chase kilometers: slow down at doors, old trees, and side passages, and cut long ordinary stretches when needed.

If heat, rain, or lake crowds break the rhythm, use the metro or DiDi between Shichahai and the towers. Not every hutong street is beautiful; pass noisy stretches without regret and look for quieter parallel lanes.
content.routeLogic:
At the mansion, see the courtyards and garden before entering the lanes.
At Shichahai, walk the shore only where it is not too tight.
Use Yandai Xiejie as a short passage, not required shopping.
At the towers, decide whether to climb based on energy and visibility.
Finish in Wudaoying where it is easy to reach metro or taxi.
content.bestFor:
the day after palaces and museums
old lanes, water, and short pauses
photos along the way without a heavy museum plan
content.notFor:
travelers who need even sidewalks all day
heavy rain
crowds in narrow streets
content.beforeYouGo:
For a long walk, choose April-May or September-October when possible.
In summer, move long walking stretches to morning or evening.
On weekends the lakes and Yandai Xiejie are tighter; keep parallel lanes in mind.
content.timing: Start before lunch at Prince Kung Mansion. Reach the lakes when you want open air, leave the towers for the moment you need orientation, and end in Wudaoying as a calmer pre-dinner finish.
content.fallbackPlan: If the lanes wear you down, take a taxi from Shichahai to the towers. In heavy rain, keep only the mansion, a covered meal, and a short walk by the water.

content.context: Inside the mansion, walk the courtyards first, then the garden and rocks. The neighboring hutongs make more sense after that. If tour groups crowd the narrow passages, do not stand behind them; move through the garden edges.
content.lookFor:
Sequence of courtyards
Garden rocks
Changes between enclosed and open spaces
content.practical:
Morning works best.
It is easy to stay longer than planned.
If dense groups block the way, use the garden edges.
content.nextMove: Walk toward Shichahai, choosing quieter lanes between the larger streets.

stopList.name: Shichahai
stopList.note: Stay by the water until it gets tight

content.context: At Shichahai, walk along the shore and choose the side by shade and crowd density. If the waterfront gets loud or rickshaws crowd the lane, step into a nearby alley and return to the lake after a few turns.
content.lookFor:
Bridges and reflections
Courtyard entrances near the water
Contrast between bars and residential alleys
content.practical:
In heat, stay on the shaded side.
Skip a full loop if the shore is packed.
Use side streets when you need a break from the crowd.
content.nextMove: Continue toward Yandai Xiejie, but do not stop at every shop.

stopList.name: Yandai Xiejie
stopList.note: Pass through briefly without shopping pressure

content.context: Yandai Xiejie is short and often packed. Treat it as the passage from water to towers: notice the bend of the street, older facades between signs, and side hutongs if the flow stops.
content.lookFor:
The curve of the street
Older facades among shop signs
Passages into side hutongs
content.practical:
Keep your bag close in dense flow.
Choose food by queue movement and freshness, not just the sign.
If the street is overloaded, use parallel lanes.
content.nextMove: Head toward the Drum and Bell Towers as the next large landmark.

stopList.name: Drum Tower
stopList.note: Decide on the spot whether to climb

content.context: At the Drum and Bell Towers, stop on the square and check the map: water stays south, hutongs surround you, and Wudaoying is farther east. Climb only if visibility is decent and you have enough energy.
content.lookFor:
Silhouettes of the two towers
The square between streets
Views over low roofs
content.practical:
The climb can be steep.
In poor visibility, the view matters less.
If you are tired, keep to the square and move on to food or metro.
content.nextMove: From the towers, walk or ride toward Wudaoying depending on weather and energy.

stopList.name: Wudaoying Hutong
stopList.note: A cafe finish with quick transport out

content.context: In Wudaoying, slow down and look beyond cafes: side passages, old doors, and low roofs still matter. If the lane feels too commercial, turn onto a neighboring street and return to the metro from there.
content.lookFor:
Old doors
Low roofs
Mix of cafes and everyday life
content.practical:
Evenings can be tight.
Do not enter private courtyards without a clear invitation.
If the area feels too commercial, move to nearby streets.
content.nextMove: Finish at the metro or take DiDi to dinner in another district.
